seeds.rationale: Camunda single-tenant mode default — the materializer must NOT auto-seed tenantIdVar from the universal prologue. When a per-scenario step seeds it (e.g. a producer like createTenant or a chained consumer that extracts the response), the seed-rules.json catch-all id rule mints a unique tenant id. When no step seeds it, ctx['tenantIdVar'] stays undefined and the consuming request omits the tenantId field, letting the REST Gateway apply the default tenant (#342). Replaces the legacy `<default>` sentinel + multipart-strip mechanism, which sent a literal sentinel on the wire and broke re-runnability for producer ops.
